steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence lists three programming languages (COBOL, Java, Clojure), each followed by a descriptive phrase containing internal commas. When list items have internal commas, semicolons must separate the items to prevent ambiguity.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — '1959, Java developed by James Gosling in 1995; and Clojure': uses a comma after 1959 where a semicolon is needed to mark the boundary between COBOL and Java. Also drops the comma after “Java” that introduces Gosling's description. ✗
  • Choice B — '1959, Java; developed by James Gosling in 1995; and Clojure,': uses a comma after 1959 (wrong—needs semicolon), and the semicolon after “Java” incorrectly splits “Java” from its own descriptive phrase. ✗
  • Choice C — '1959; Java, developed by James Gosling in 1995, and Clojure,': correctly places a semicolon after 1959 to separate COBOL from Java, but then uses only a comma before “and Clojure”—a semicolon is required there too since all three items contain internal commas. ✗
  • Choice D — '1959; Java, developed by James Gosling in 1995; and Clojure,': semicolons after both 1959 and 1995 correctly separate all three list items, while commas within each item's descriptive phrase are preserved. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
